eligible_applicant_type: community-based organizations with a fiscal sponsor that is a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit
project_area: Urban agriculture (community gardens, nonprofit gardens, food forests) for purchasing tools, equipment, seeds, seedlings, compost, and other garden supplies for communal use
demographic_requirements: None specified beyond organizational eligibility (501(c)(3) status or fiscal sponsorship)
eligibility_summary: Open to registered 501(c)(3) nonprofits or community-based organizations partnering with a fiscal sponsor that is a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit. Applicants may apply for up to $2,000 per garden or food forest site.
